meta.subject: English Language
meta.philosophy: The English Language Syllabus for ECE Levels 1 and 2 is designed to help children aged three to five develop listening, speaking, reading and writing. The syllabus has been developed to ensure consistent exposure to and practice of language skills to become fluent and successful in academics as they progress to Grade 1 and beyond. Section B contains content for Level 2 (4-5 years).
meta.competence_learning: The teaching of Pre-Literacy and Language will follow the recommended approach of teaching key phonological and phonemic awareness, phonics, vocabulary, listening comprehension. Generally, lessons delivery should follow the Gradual Release of Responsibility Model (GRR) where the teacher models the activity, then doing the activity with the learners, and finally learners doing the activity independently. A variety of teaching techniques should be employed to meet different learning styles.
goals: Analyse stories and comprehend and interpret pictures
Demonstrate understanding that speech is made up of sounds
Use appropriate language in different situations
Demonstrate good listening, speaking, reading and writing skills
Interpret messages represented by signs and symbols
Take turns when conversing with others
Recognise and demonstrate understanding of letter-sound relationship

specific_outcomes: Use appropriate greetings at different times of the day
Respond to common greetings (e.g. how are you? I am fine...)
Use common greetings at different times of the day (good morning, good afternoon, good evening, good night)
Discuss importance of taking turns to speak to communicate effectively and clearly
Practise taking turns speaking, sharing ideas one at a time using talking token
Role-play taking turns to speak (How are you? Thank you, I am alright/fine...)
Follow simple instructions (close the door, open the book, keep quiet, sit properly)
Role-play following instructions
Discuss classroom rules (peacefully, pick litter, keep things)
Ask for permission (Teacher, may I...? Excuse me, teacher sir/madam...)
Respond to permission given (thank you, alright sir/madam)
Name objects found at home (Kitchen: broom, spoon; Sitting room: TV, table, sofa; Bedroom: mat, bed, blanket, dressing mirror; Toilet/bathroom: dustbin, face towel, bathtub)
Name objects found at school (classroom: chair, table, blackboard, whiteboard, bookshelf, pencil, board duster; outside: national flag, trees, grass, classroom, toilets, playground)
Talk about domestic animals (dog, cat, cow, goat)
Mould and name animals
Talk about wild animals (Elephant, Giraffe, Monkey)
Discuss different occupations (teacher, nurse, doctor, footballer, netballer, driver)
Role-play various occupations found in the community
Name various modes of transport (car, aeroplane, train)
Describe objects according to primary colours: red, yellow, blue
Sort objects according to primary colours
Describe objects according to secondary colours: green, orange, purple
Sort objects according to secondary colours
Describe things according to their attributes (size, weight, e.g. big, small, long, short)
Describe home chores and practices (sweeping, washing, cleaning, cooking)
Role-play home chores
Discuss school rules using appropriate language (be in line, listen attentively, quietly)
Describe positions of things using prepositions (in, under)
Identify things by quantity: one/many
Express nouns as singular and plural (book-books, desk-desks, chair-chairs)
Name days of the week by naming and singing (Sunday, Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday)
Name months of the year by naming and singing (January to December)
Sing 'Care for the Body' song (this is the way we...)
Name items used to keep the body clean (soap, water, toothpaste, face cloth, comb, toothbrush)
Use appropriate language to talk about road safety rules (right, left, look right, across, robots...)
Identify simple events (birthday parties, Christmas, New Year)
Narrate simple events using where, when, how
Mention different weather patterns (rainy, windy, sunny)
Talk about the weather of the day/month (windy day...)
Use appropriate language to tell stories about caring for others
Listen to simple oral stories about caring for others
Retell simple oral stories about caring for others
Role-play caring for others (sick, elderly, differently abled)
Identify and discuss language used for various means of communication (telephones, radio, newspaper)
Tell simple stories using means of communication
Identify and discuss appropriate vocabulary for buying and selling (how much, change)
Dramatise/role-play selling and buying
Demonstrate left to right eye movement by tracking with finger, reading pointer sticks to follow lines from left to right, singing and moving from left to right
Match similar things from left to right (pictures, objects, patterns, numbers, shapes, colour)
Match items according to relationship (car-road, water-mother, baby-egg)
Identify and sort items with similar properties by shape, length
Odd One Out: pick what is different from given shapes and items (circle, triangle, square)
Read picture stories by arranging a set of pictures representing key elements of a story in order they happened
Recognise sounds: name domestic animals and associate them to the sounds they produce (cow-moo, dog-woof)
Listen, watch and name recorded domestic and wild animals
Name the musical instruments producing sound
Identify and imitate sounds heard
Dramatise blind fold voice recognition
Sound out initial sounds of consonants in given words (e.g. s-at, t-il, p-at, n-ot) using sat, at
Count the number of sounds in regular words (e.g. sit = 3 sounds)
Blend sounds: putting sounds together (e.g. blending: s-a-t = sat, s-i-t = sit)
Manipulate sounds within words to form new words (e.g. s-at → s-it)
Identify positions of sounds in words (e.g. initial: p-i-f is found at beginning of the word)
Play games or sing to identify rhyming words
Recite short alliterative tongue twisters (big blue balloon... pink pigs play)
Demonstrate correct sitting posture (Leo, Jumbo, Kalulu sitting upright, resting on desk, feet flat on the floor)
Practise finger and hand exercises: tapping, stretches, finger circles, turning, drawing
Demonstrate correct book handling habits (hold books gently, shelve books away from water, no chewing of paper or pages, use book covers)
Explore books (turning pages, feeling texture, identifying pictures)
Make own picture book using plain papers to draw stories
Handle different gadgets, demonstrate how and explore their purpose (phone, mini tablet, computers)
Hold pencil using the tripod grip (pencil rests against the hand and the middle finger while thumb and index finger control the pencil)
Draw patterns: horizontal, vertical, slanting left, slanting in all directions
Copy simple words using letters already learned
Copy short sentences (e.g. The cat sat...)
Copy own name from name card (e.g. Joy)
Name members of the extended family
Tell their relationship with extended family members
Name objects in the school environment (inside classroom: pencils, chairs; outside classroom: flag, dustbin)
Talk about functions of familiar objects at school
Name different occupations in the community
Role-play different occupations in the community
Classify domestic animals according to functions (food, transport, farming, pets)
Name common wild animals

topics.topic_title: Stories, Rhymes, Prayers and Instructions - Listening and Speaking
specific_outcomes: Listen to a simple story told (fairy tales, legends)
Listen to a simple story that is read out
Retell a simple story
Tell own stories
Act out a simple story
Recite a simple rhyme
Recite simple tongue twisters
Say a simple prayer before a meal, bedtime, before school
Give simple instructions (e.g. pick flowers, open the book, knock on the door)
Give simple directions (go straight, turn left)
Talk about things that make them happy or sad
Talk about means of communication and appropriate vocabulary (mobile phones)
Act out family experiences (shopping, going to church, collecting fruits)
Take turns sharing experiences with the class (experiences with friends, e.g. games)
Talk about home chores and school chores
Talk about modes of transport
Role-play buying and selling

topics.topic_title: Messages, Riddles and Poems - Listening and Speaking
specific_outcomes: Interpret common signposts (e.g. girls, boys, dog)
Say and respond to simple riddles
Recite simple poems
Identify parts of a book (front, back cover, pages, spine of a book)
Interpret pictures (picture trips)
Sequence pictures to form a picture strip
Identify syllables in simple oral words
Blend syllables to form simple oral words
Segment simple oral words into syllables
Identify initial sounds in oral words
Sound out letters
Respond orally to questions based on a simple story (characters and setting)
Blend sounds/letters to form syllables (consonant + vowel)
Read syllables (consonant + vowel)
Blend syllables to form simple words (vowel + consonant + vowel)
Read simple words (VC, VCV, CVC, CVCV)
Write simple patterns
Trace lines
Trace simple shapes
Trace letters
Copy simple patterns
Copy simple shapes
Copy letters
Copy syllables (consonant + vowel)
Write own name independently
Copy simple words (VC, CVC, CVCV)
Colour shapes
Colour drawings
Draw different objects
Draw to communicate ideas
